question: Will Belal Muhammad be the UFC Welterweight Champion on December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the official UFC Welterweight division champion on December 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

Only official UFC division champions will count. Interim champions will not count.

If the relevant belt is vacant at this market’s check time, or there is otherwise no champion in the specified division, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/athletes).

events.title: Who will be UFC Welterweight champion at the end of 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the official UFC Welterweight division champion on December 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

Only official UFC division champions will count. Interim champions will not count.

If the relevant belt is vacant at this market’s check time, or there is otherwise no champion in the specified division, this market will resolve to “Other”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Islam Makhachev at 63% to claim UFC welterweight gold by end-2026, driven by his unparalleled 15-fight win streak as lightweight champion and speculation he'll move up after dominating Dustin Poirier at UFC 302, leveraging Dagestani wrestling against aging or unproven 170-pounders. Carlos Prates' 24.5% reflects his blistering four-fight UFC knockout streak, including finishes over Neil Magny and Li Jingliang, positioning him as a dark horse striker. Ian Machado Garry's 18% stems from his perfect 15-0 record and stylistic win over Geoff Neal, while Michael Morales (8%) gains from prospect hype post-17-0 run. Belal Muhammad's title reign after dethroning Leon Edwards at UFC 304 holds low traction at 1%, amid doubts on his finishing power and upcoming tests like Shavkat Rakhmonov.

events.title: Who will be UFC Middleweight champion at the end of 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the official UFC Middleweight division champion on December 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

Only official UFC division champions will count. Interim champions will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Khamzat Chimaev at 67% implied probability to hold the UFC Middleweight title by end of 2026, driven by his undefeated 13-0 record, explosive grappling and striking, and clear path via the UFC 308 co-main against Robert Whittaker on October 26, where a win earns a shot at champion Dricus du Plessis. Du Plessis bolstered his credentials with a second-round submission of Israel Adesanya at UFC 305, yet traders discount his 7.9% odds amid stylistic vulnerabilities. Sean Strickland's 18.7% reflects his elite boxing and wrestling after a title loss to Du Plessis, while Nassourdine Imavov's 12% stems from his eight-fight win streak and knockout power versus aging contenders. Recent health recoveries position Chimaev for dominance through 2026.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tom Aspinall's dominance as UFC interim heavyweight champion, highlighted by his one-minute knockout of Curtis Blaydes at UFC 304, anchors trader consensus at 53.5% for him to hold the undisputed title by end-2026, amid Jon Jones' prolonged inactivity and injury recovery delaying unification. Ciryl Gane's elite striking and recent submission win over Serghei Spivac fuel his 31.5% share, positioning him as a top alternative if Aspinall falters. Dark horses like Derrick Lewis and Sergei Pavlovich draw support from raw knockout power, while rising grapplers Jailton Almeida and Spivac benefit from heavyweight momentum, though Jones' potential retirement or legacy fights introduce uncertainty to the division's path.

question: GOP uses 'Nuclear Option' to break filibuster by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the US Senate passes a motion to invoke cloture and end debate on any part of the legislative process, with such a cloture motion receiving Yea votes from fewer than three-fifths of the Senators by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve "No".

Confirmations are considered part of the Senate’s “advice and consent” function, not part of the Senate’s “legislative process,” and will therefore not count towards this market's resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the U.S. government.

events.title: Will the GOP use 'Nuclear Option' to break filibuster by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the US Senate passes a motion to invoke cloture and end debate on any part of the legislative process, with such a cloture motion receiving Yea votes from fewer than three-fifths of the Senators by October 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve "No".

Confirmations are considered part of the Senate’s “advice and consent” function, not part of the Senate’s “legislative process,” and will therefore not count towards this market's resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the U.S. government.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket leans heavily against Republicans invoking the nuclear option to alter filibuster rules by the market's deadline, with Yes shares trading below 30%, reflecting GOP Senate divisions amid their 53-47 majority post-2024 elections. Key resistance stems from outgoing Leader Mitch McConnell's longstanding defense of the 60-vote cloture threshold and incoming Leader John Thune's caution, despite President-elect Trump's public calls to eliminate it for swift agenda passage via reconciliation. Recent catalysts include Trump's Truth Social posts urging reform and supportive rhetoric from senators like Josh Hawley, but no formal leadership commitment. Watch January 2025 Senate organization for rules votes that could shift odds.

question: Will Reza Pahlavi enter Iran by June 30?
description:
If Reza Pahlavi visits Iran between market creation and June 30, 2026 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Reza Pahlavi physically entering the terrestrial territory of Iran. Whether or not Reza Pahlavi enters Iranian airspace or maritime territory during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will Reza Pahlavi enter Iran by...?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket implies low odds for Reza Pahlavi entering Iran by the market deadline, driven by the exiled son's lack of announced travel plans amid the regime's longstanding arrest warrant against him and severe risks for opposition figures. Recent developments include Pahlavi's July 2024 meetings with Israeli leaders and calls for Iranian uprising from abroad, amplifying his symbolic role in protest movements like Woman, Life, Freedom, but without feasible entry pathways under current border controls. No confirmed diplomatic shifts or safe passage negotiations exist, though escalating domestic unrest or international sanctions could alter dynamics; watch for regime announcements or Pahlavi statements ahead of key protest anniversaries.

question: Will 6+ coins launched in 2026 end the year above $1B FDV?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if 6 or more tokens launched in 2026 end the year with an FDV above $1B. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

The token must be actively and publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be calculated by multiplying the total token supply by the token price. The token price used will be the “Close” price on CoinGecko for December 31, 2026, as shown in the token’s historical data (e.g., Hyperliquid: https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/hyperliquid/historical_data).

Stablecoins, LSTs, liquidity pool tokens, or synthetic representations of other assets will not qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Islam Makhachev leads trader consensus at 63% implied probability as the current UFC pound-for-pound king, bolstered by his dominant decision win over Arman Tsarukyan at UFC 308, extending his lightweight title reign to five defenses amid talks of lightweight supremacy or a welterweight leap. Ilia Topuria's 32.5% share surges post his third-round knockout of Max Holloway in the same event, prompting his featherweight title vacancy and bold lightweight challenge targeting Makhachev, signaling superfight potential. Tom Aspinall (26.7%) gains from interim heavyweight dominance and Jon Jones injury delays, while Alexandre Pantoja (26.2%) holds steady via multiple flyweight defenses despite the division's lighter P4P weight. Recent momentum, title vacancies, and cross-division ambitions shape long-term 2026 positioning amid inherent ranking volatility.

question: Record crypto liquidation in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if a total crypto liquidations event exceeds the highest liquidation amount ever recorded ($19.16B) at any point in 2026, as tracked by CoinGlass. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source will be CoinGlass liquidation data (https://www.coinglass.com/LiquidationData), specifically the “Top 10 Crypto Liquidation Events of All Time.”

If CoinGlass data becomes permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting will be used.

question: Israel x Hamas ceasefire cancelled by June 30?
description:
On October 9, 2025 Israel and Hamas signed a deal implementing a ceasefire: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/israel-hamas-agree-gaza-ceasefire-return-hostages-2025-10-09/

This market will resolve to "Yes" if a either Israel or Hamas announce the cancellation of the ongoing ceasefire, or if a consensus of credible reporting confirms that the ceasefire is no longer in effect, by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

Announcements that one side or another violated the ongoing ceasefire will not be enough to resolve this market to "Yes"; only a definitive announcement by Israel or Hamas, or otherwise a wide consensus of credible reporting that that the ceasefire has been ended, will qualify to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary solution resolution source will be official information from Hamas or the government of Israel, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the Israel-Hamas ceasefire outcome hinges on stalled Qatar-mediated talks, with Israel rejecting Hamas' mid-October 2024 counterproposal demanding full troop withdrawal and war end, while insisting on resume-rights for operations against Hamas re-arming. Netanyahu's office confirmed review but stressed destroying Hamas' capabilities remains priority amid daily Gaza strikes killing dozens. Hamas accuses Israel of sabotage via escalated raids. No active ceasefire exists since March, amplifying deadlock risks. Upcoming U.S. election rhetoric, potential new proposals, and UN Security Council sessions could influence probabilities, reflecting traders' consensus on mutual intransigence from historical patterns.

question: Will Russia capture all of Donetsk Oblast by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia militarily captures the entirety of Donetsk Oblast by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET.

Donetsk Oblast will be considered captured when the entirety of the below-specified municipalities is simultaneously shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If those areas are not simultaneously shaded red by the resolution date, this market will resolve to “No”.

The relevant municipalities in Donetsk Oblast that must be simultaneously captured in their entirety are: Kramatorsk, Sloviansk, Kostiantynivka, Druzhkivka, Pokrovsk, Dobropillia, Oleksandrivka, Cherkaske, and Sviatohirsk.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

The territory of a municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities with a darker grey line. If all area within a municipality is shaded in red, however, the shading does not precisely match up with the border such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this may still qualify.

Once Russia has captured the entirety of the above-specified municipalities simultaneously, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap is rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: 

1. Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

2. If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will not qualify, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. Similarly, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Control must be established through military conquest.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces' incremental advances in Donetsk Oblast, including the recent capture of Vuhledar and pressure on Kurakhove and Pokrovsk logistics hubs, form the core driver of trader consensus on timelines for full territorial control. These gains reflect Moscow's manpower and artillery superiority amid Ukraine's ammunition constraints and recruitment challenges, though Kyiv retains roughly 20-25% of the oblast with fortified lines. Traders monitor the grinding pace—about 1-2 km per week—against winter conditions that historically slow operations. Key upcoming events include intensified fighting around Pokrovsk and potential US aid adjustments post-November election, introducing volatility to implied probabilities.

question: NATO x Russia military clash by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of a NATO country and Russia between January 2, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between NATO and Russian military forces. Non-violent actions, such as airspace violations, firing of warning shots (such as the June, 2021 Black Sea Confrontations between Russian forces and HMS Defender), or cyberattacks will not qualify.

Interception of missiles or other one-way attack or loitering munitions (e.g. Shahed drones) which are targeting a 3rd party other than the listed countries or their respective forces will not alone qualify. Shooting down UAVs which are not munitions (e.g. MQ-9, Orlan 10, Orion, Bayraktar TB2, etc.) will qualify.

Intentional physical collisions, including aerial interceptions and naval ramming without the direct use of weaponry, such as the 2023 Black Sea incident—where a Russian Su-27 damaged a U.S. MQ-9 Reaper drone's propeller, leading to its crash— will not qualify regardless of damage.

Military contractors will qualify only if confirmed to be operating under the direct command or coordination of the respective state’s armed forces (e.g. the Battle of Khasham would not qualify).

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a NATO-Russia military clash at low implied probabilities, primarily due to robust deterrence from NATO's collective defense pact and Russia's nuclear posture amid the protracted Ukraine conflict. Recent developments include Russia's July 2024 security pact with North Korea, Ukraine's Kursk incursion prompting Moscow's warnings of NATO "red lines," and escalated Baltic Sea aerial incidents without escalation. No verified direct combat has occurred, with Western leaders stressing indirect support for Kyiv via aid packages. Upcoming catalysts like the NATO Washington summit in late July and US election dynamics on Ukraine funding could shift risks, though diplomatic backchannels persist to avert direct confrontation.

question: Will anyone charged over daycare fraud in MN be deported by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of any individual in connection with any alleged fake daycare facility in Minnesota, and the charged person is officially deported from the United States by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market, the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

Deportation is defined as the formal removal of a person from the US by government authorities due to legal or administrative reasons. Voluntary departure, extradition, or relocation without an official deportation order will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Will anyone charged over daycare fraud in MN be deported by March 31?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors "No" at 97.2% implied probability due to the glacial pace of U.S. criminal and immigration processes, far outstripping the March 31 deadline following October 2024 charges against 46 individuals in Minnesota's $250 million fake daycare Medicaid fraud scheme. Most defendants, primarily Somali immigrants with legal status like green cards or asylum, face months of pretrial proceedings, potential trials, sentencing, and then lengthy ICE removal hearings—often 1-2 years total. Recent court filings show no deportation holds yet, reinforcing trader confidence. Realistic upsets include an undocumented defendant pleading guilty quickly with expedited ICE action, though none identified publicly shifts odds meaningfully.

question: Will Trump pardon Martin Shkreli before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual receives a presidential pardon, commutation, or reprieve from Donald Trump between January 2, 2026 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for whether the individual is pardoned or not will be official information from the US government, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Who will Trump pardon before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual receives a presidential pardon, commutation, or reprieve from Donald Trump between November 17, 2025 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for whether the individual is pardoned or not will be official information from the US government, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President-elect Trump's post-election statements signaling intent to review and pardon non-violent January 6th defendants convicted in federal cases represent the dominant driver of trader consensus, with markets pricing high implied probabilities for that outcome before 2027. In a December Time magazine interview, Trump affirmed he would "absolutely" examine those cases, echoing his first-term use of clemency for allies like Roger Stone amid over 1,500 related convictions. Incoming Attorney General nominee Pam Bondi has voiced support for such reviews. His pardon authority activates upon January 20, 2025, inauguration, though exact targets, timing, and scope remain uncertain amid ongoing DOJ transitions and potential legal hurdles.

question: Over 4 coins launched in 2026 end the year in the top 100?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, at 11:59 PM ET on December 31, 2026, the number of coins launched in 2026 that appear in the top 100 by market capitalization on CoinGecko is greater than the number specified in the title. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source is CoinGecko’s Top 100 market cap rankings (https://www.coingecko.com/).

Stablecoins, liquid staking tokens (LSTs), liquidity pool tokens, and synthetic representations of other assets will not qualify.

events.title: How many coins launched in 2026 end the year in the top 100?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, at 11:59 PM ET on December 31, 2026, the number of coins launched in 2026 that appear in the top 100 by market capitalization on CoinGecko is greater than the number specified in the title. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source is CoinGecko’s Top 100 market cap rankings (https://www.coingecko.com/).

Stablecoins, liquid staking tokens (LSTs), liquidity pool tokens, and synthetic representations of other assets will not qualify.

question: Jerome Powell out as Fed Chair by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Jerome Powell ceases to be Chair of the Federal Reserve for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Jerome Powell's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Jerome Powell and the Federal Reserve; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Jerome Powell out as Fed Chair by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Jerome Powell ceases to be the Chair of the U.S. Federal Reserve for any period of time between this market's creation and the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the U.S. Government; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a low probability for Jerome Powell exiting as Fed Chair before his May 2026 term end, anchored by Federal Reserve independence norms and President-elect Trump's December 2024 Time interview stating no plans to fire him, citing risks to financial markets. Legal hurdles from court rulings require "cause" for removal, deterring abrupt action. Powell's December FOMC press conference reiterated the Fed's apolitical mandate amid rate cut debates. Key catalysts ahead include Trump's January 20 inauguration, Treasury secretary confirmation, and January 28-29 FOMC meeting, where appointee signals or policy clashes could shift odds, though historical precedents favor full terms for chairs.

question: Utah State Aggies vs. Colorado State Rams
description:
In the upcoming CBB game, scheduled for January 9 at 12:00 AM ET:

If the Utah State Aggies win, the market will resolve to "Utah State Aggies".

If the Colorado State Rams win, the market will resolve to "Colorado State Rams".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

events.title: Utah State Aggies vs. Colorado State Rams
events.description:
This event is for the CBB game between Utah State Aggies and Colorado State Rams on January 9 at 12:00 AM ET.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Utah State Aggies hold a slim 52.5% implied probability edge over Colorado State Rams in this Mountain West Conference football matchup, driven by the Aggies' explosive passing game under QB Levi Williams (2,400+ yards, 20 TDs) offsetting their porous defense that concedes 38 points per game amid a 1-6 skid. Competitive balance stems from CSU's solid 4-2 home record at Canvas Stadium, gritty rushing attack (No. 25 nationally), and recent momentum from a 24-17 upset over then-No. 24 UNLV, keeping head-to-heads tight (split last four). Official injury reports show clean bills for both starters; odds could flip toward Rams if Williams faces pressure up front, or Aggies if CSU's secondary falters on the road test.

question: Venezuela election scheduled by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the next Venezuelan presidential election is scheduled by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

To qualify, the election must be scheduled to be held before 2030.

This market is about whether a date for the next Venezuelan presidential election is announced within this market's timeframe. Whether the election is supposed to take place within this market's timeframe or later will have no effect on the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the government of Venezuela; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Venezuelan National Electoral Council (CNE), controlled by allies of President Nicolás Maduro, has not announced a new presidential election date after the disputed July 28, 2024, vote, where Maduro was declared winner despite opposition claims of fraud backed by independent tallies showing rival Edmundo González ahead. Maduro's January 10, 2025, inauguration extends his term through 2030, solidifying government control amid suppressed protests and opposition leaders in exile. International skepticism from the US, EU, and OAS has prompted sanctions but no enforcement for a redo. Trader consensus reflects low odds for near-term rescheduling, prioritizing Maduro's institutional dominance over economic woes or diplomatic pressure; watch for CNE statements or regional votes that could signal shifts.

question: Cilia Flores released from custody by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Cilia Flores is released from custody by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Cilia Flores is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Cilia Flores is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Cilia Flores to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". 

Temporary outings from prison for purposes such as testifying in court or before Congress, while still under the custody of correctional authorities, will not count as a release.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects heavy skepticism toward claims of Cilia Flores, wife of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro and National Assembly president, being held in custody, with odds favoring no release event due to absence of credible confirmation from official Venezuelan sources or international monitors. No primary announcements or verifiable reports substantiate her detention amid post-July 2024 election crackdowns targeting opposition figures like Edmundo González aides, not regime insiders like Flores. Recent PSUV legislative maneuvers and Maduro's consolidation reinforce her prominent role. Traders watch for U.S. sanctions updates or OAS diplomatic pressure, but rapid Venezuela political shifts underscore inherent uncertainty in such niche outcomes.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Nicolás Maduro of Venezuela remains securely in power and has never been taken into custody, anchoring trader consensus toward negligible odds of any "release" scenario on this market. Recent U.S. sanctions and OAS condemnations add diplomatic pressure but no enforcement mechanism for arrest. Upcoming National Assembly sessions and potential ICC probes could influence sentiment, though regime stability favors status quo pricing.

question: Maduro guilty of all counts?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Nicolás Maduro is found guilty of all counts in the indictment 'UNITED STATES OF AMERICA V. NICOLAS MADURO MOROS[...]' (S4 11 Cr. 205 (AKH)) by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve based on the initial verdict rendered by the court regarding Maduro’s indictment as of market creation (https://www.justice.gov/opa/media/1422326/dl).

If Nicolás Maduro is not found guilty of all counts in the referenced indictment by the resolution time, this market will resolve to “No.” This includes any outcome in which no trial occurs, or that does not result in a guilty verdict or a court-accepted guilty plea on all counts, such as acquittal on any count, partial conviction, mistrial, hung jury, or dismissal of any count.

The market will resolve according to the initial verdict rendered in this case. Any appeals will have no bearing on the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the U.S. court system; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket leans heavily against Nicolás Maduro being found guilty of all counts in his ongoing US federal indictment, with "No" shares reflecting 68.5% implied probability, primarily due to the absence of custody, extradition, or any scheduled trial despite the 2020 Southern District of New York charges on narcoterrorism, drug trafficking conspiracy, and related offenses. Maduro's firm control over Venezuelan institutions, reinforced by the supreme court's validation of his disputed July 2024 reelection amid opposition protests and international non-recognition from the US and others, diminishes near-term conviction prospects. Recent US sanctions extensions and bounty renewals signal pressure but no judicial progress, underscoring geopolitical barriers shaping these odds.

question: Will Nicolás Maduro be sentenced to no prison time?
description:
Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro was indicted in the Southern District of New York on January 3, 2026 on charges including Narco-Terrorism Conspiracy and Cocaine Importation Conspiracy. Maduro was indicted alongside alleged co-conspirators in an indictment titled "United States of America V. Nicolás Maduro Moros, Diosdado Cabello Rondón, Ramón Rodríguez Chacín, Cilia Adela Flores de Maduro, Nicolás Ernesto Maduro Guerra".

This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Nicolas Maduro (Nicolas Maduro Moros) relating to this indictment by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Maduro is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If Maduro is sentenced to life imprisonment without a specified numerical term of years, this market will resolve to the highest range bracket.

If the imposed sentence falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Maduro is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in the first sentencing for this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the United States court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Persistent international legal pressures, including a U.S. narcotics trafficking indictment carrying potential life imprisonment and an ongoing ICC probe into human rights abuses during 2017-2021 protests, position 60+ years (38%) as the leading outcome in trader consensus for Nicolás Maduro's prison time. Recent Venezuelan election disputes, where opposition tallies showed rival Edmundo González ahead despite Maduro's official win and January 10 inauguration, have intensified non-recognition by the U.S., EU, and others, alongside fresh sanctions on regime officials. These factors elevate long-sentence odds via regime change or extradition scenarios, while "no prison time" (25%) reflects Maduro's current military-backed control amid suppressed dissent, though uncertainty looms over diplomatic talks and protests.

question: Will Milady floor price reach 6 ETH before 2027?
description:
This market will immediately resolve to “Yes” if the Milady floor price reaches 6 ETH or higher at any point between December 31, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market will be NftPriceFloor, specifically the "Milady price chart" available at https://nftpricefloor.com/milady, with "1M" selected on the top bar. The price will be determined based on the value shown when hovering over the chart.

Only data from NFTPriceFloor will be used to resolve this market. Prices from other data providers or NFT marketplaces will not be considered.

question: Will Venezuela become 51st state?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" any portion of Venezuela is formally annexed into the United States of America and becomes the 51st state between January 3, 2026, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An official agreement that is announced will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when it is slated to go into effect. If any territory that includes no portion of Venezuelan land is made the 51st US state within this market's timeframe, this market will immediately resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the government of the United States, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 96.6% for Venezuela becoming the 51st US state, driven by insurmountable constitutional barriers under Article IV requiring congressional approval and territorial status—Venezuela remains a sovereign UN member with no US jurisdiction. Geopolitical realities compound this: Nicolás Maduro's disputed July 2024 election victory, backed by military and allies like Russia and China, entrenches regime control despite US sanctions and opposition claims of fraud favoring Edmundo González. No credible US policy proposals for annexation exist, amid international law prohibitions on forcible integration. Realistic shifts would demand improbable events like full US military intervention or a pro-integration Venezuelan plebiscite, which traders dismiss given zero momentum.

question: Will Edmundo González be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa.  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

Recent catalysts include regime suppression of protests, PSUV internal alignments positioning Rodríguez as successor, and uncertainty over U.S. policy shifts under incoming Trump administration.

question: Will OpTic Texas finish in the top 4 of the CDL Regular Season?
description:
This is a market to predict which teams finish in the top 4 of the Call of Duty League Regular Season standings for the 2025–26 season.

If the listed team officially finishes in the top 4 of the Call of Duty League standings at the end of the season, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If two or more teams are tied on points for a top 4 position, resolution will follow the official Call of Duty League tiebreaking procedures.

If the 2025–26 Call of Duty League season is canceled or not completed before October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Call of Duty League (https://www.callofdutyleague.com/en-us/standings?utm_source=cdlweb&utm_medium=navigationbar&utm_campaign=general). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atlanta FaZe and OpTic Texas dominate trader consensus for CDL regular season top-4 finishes, holding commanding leads in CDL Points after Miami Heretics' Major 4 victory propelled them into contention while exposing Toronto Ultra's mid-pack slide. Heretics' breakout with standout AR performances from Pred has traders pricing them at strong implied probabilities for playoffs seeding, but FaZe's consistent map control and OpTic's flex sub Cellium keep them as frontrunners amid roster stability across top squads. Recent online qualifier sweeps boosted Carolina Royal Ravens into bubble talks, yet the final Major III looms as the decider, where rest advantages and head-to-head rivalries could shuffle the leaderboard before playoffs lock in direct qualification perks.

question: Will Trump pardon Nicolas Maduro before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual receives a presidential pardon, commutation, or reprieve from Donald Trump between January 2, 2026 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for whether the individual is pardoned or not will be official information from the US government, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Who will Trump pardon before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual receives a presidential pardon, commutation, or reprieve from Donald Trump between November 17, 2025 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for whether the individual is pardoned or not will be official information from the US government, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President-elect Trump's post-election statements signaling intent to review and pardon non-violent January 6th defendants convicted in federal cases represent the dominant driver of trader consensus, with markets pricing high implied probabilities for that outcome before 2027. In a December Time magazine interview, Trump affirmed he would "absolutely" examine those cases, echoing his first-term use of clemency for allies like Roger Stone amid over 1,500 related convictions. Incoming Attorney General nominee Pam Bondi has voiced support for such reviews. His pardon authority activates upon January 20, 2025, inauguration, though exact targets, timing, and scope remain uncertain amid ongoing DOJ transitions and potential legal hurdles.

question: Miguel Díaz-Canel out as leader of Cuba by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Miguel Díaz-Canel ceases to be President of Cuba for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Miguel Díaz-Canel's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Miguel Díaz-Canel and the government of Cuba; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Miguel Díaz-Canel out as leader of Cuba by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Cuba’s current leader, Miguel Díaz-Canel, is removed from power for any length of time by the listed date, ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

For the purposes of this market, Díaz-Canel will be considered to be removed from power if he resigns, is detained, or otherwise ceases to hold (or is publicly reported to be unable to perform the duties of) the office of First Secretary of the Communist Party of Cuba, which is widely regarded as Cuba’s top political post, within this market’s timeframe. 

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a low implied probability for Miguel Díaz-Canel's removal as Cuba's president soon, driven by the Cuban Communist Party's firm control despite deepening economic woes. Chronic blackouts, fuel shortages, and food scarcity—worsened by U.S. sanctions and Hurricane Rafael's recent devastation—have sparked public discontent and online criticism, echoing 2021 protests that were swiftly suppressed. However, no verified signs of internal party fractures, elite defections, or external interventions have emerged, with Díaz-Canel securely re-elected in 2023 for a term through 2028. Upcoming National Assembly sessions could signal policy shifts, but leadership stability remains the baseline amid regime resilience.

Petro will be considered to be removed from power if he resigns, is detained, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as President of Colombia within this market's timeframe. 

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Gustavo Petro's removal as Colombia's president at low odds for near-term dates, reflecting constitutional protections and his congressional coalition despite approval ratings hovering around 34%. Key drivers include stalled reforms sparking nationwide strikes, scandals implicating son Nicolás in money laundering, and recent congressional probes into Petro's undisclosed October urinary surgery and family hires. Opposition motions for impeachment over health secrecy gained traction but lack votes for accusation. Term ends August 2026; watch December sessions for escalation, though historical precedents favor incumbents surviving challenges.

question: US forces in Venezuela again by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Venezuela at any point between market creation and the specified date, 11:59 PM ET.

Military special operation forces will qualify; however, intelligence operatives will not count. 

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Venezuela to qualify. Entering Venezuela's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Venezuela for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: US forces enter Venezuela again by...?
events.description:

Military special operation forces will qualify; however, intelligence operatives will not count. 

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Venezuela to qualify. Entering Venezuela's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Venezuela for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a very low probability of US forces entering Venezuela, driven by the absence of any official military signals despite post-election tensions after Nicolas Maduro's disputed July 28 victory claim. US responses have centered on sanctions against Maduro allies, recognition of opposition leader Edmundo Gonzalez's tally, and State Department calls for peaceful resolution, explicitly avoiding intervention amid domestic priorities like the Ukraine and Middle East conflicts. Recent Venezuelan crackdowns on protesters and Essequibo border rhetoric with Guyana heighten risks, but US policy emphasizes diplomacy. The November 5 presidential election looms as a potential catalyst for hawkish shifts under a new administration.

question: Will María Corina Machado enter Venezuela by March 31?
description:

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as María Corina Machado physically entering the terrestrial territory of Venezuela. Whether or not María Corina Machado enters Venezuelan airspace or maritime territory during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on María Corina Machado's potential return to Venezuela hinges on the Maduro regime's tightened border controls and opposition restrictions following the disputed July 2024 election, where she led challenges to official results. Recent Supreme Tribunal rulings upholding her political ban and passport revocation have heightened risks, while U.S. sanctions and OAS condemnations add diplomatic pressure but limited leverage. Machado, operating from exile since late 2024 threats escalated, has rallied international support via virtual addresses. Upcoming opposition rallies on January 10—Maduro's inauguration date—and potential Interpol developments could shift probabilities, underscoring the opposition's strategy amid regime crackdowns reflected in market pricing.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the buyback tracker available at https://fees.pump.fun/, specifically “Total $PUMP Purchases (USD)”.

question: US strike on Colombia by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a US-initiated drone, missile, or air strike on the soil of Colombia is announced or credibly reported to have occurred by the listed date ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including FPV and ATGM strikes as well as cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by any United States operatives, including military forces, intelligence agencies, or other U.S. government operatives, that physically impact ground territory within the listed country.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory (including rivers, lakes, ports, but excluding territorial sea) of the listed country counts.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, ground incursions, naval shelling, or cyberattacks will not qualify.

Any strike occurring during this market’s timeframe that is claimed by either Donald Trump or the U.S. government will qualify.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

This market will remain open until the end of the second day after the resolution time. If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by that time, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders price a US military strike on Colombia at near-zero implied probability, reflecting the nations' longstanding alliance as a major non-NATO partner with deep security cooperation against drug cartels and Venezuelan instability. Recent tensions under President Petro—over oil drilling pauses and ELN peace talks—have strained diplomacy but prompted no credible escalation signals from US officials, who prioritize joint counter-narcotics efforts. No primary sources indicate strike preparations, and historical precedents favor sanctions or aid adjustments over force. Upcoming Petro-Trump transition dynamics post-January 2025 could test ties, but markets see diplomatic channels prevailing amid mutual strategic interests.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on potential US military strikes in Mexico shows low implied probabilities, driven by historical US aversion to operations on Mexican soil amid sovereignty tensions and alliance strains. President-elect Trump's Time interview statements vowing to bomb drug cartels on day one fueled brief speculation, but lack official policy details or congressional buy-in before his January 20 inauguration. Mexico's President Sheinbaum rejected intervention, warning of retaliation, while no Biden-era actions materialized despite fentanyl crisis rhetoric. Traders weigh diplomatic fallout against border security pressures; watch confirmation hearings for Trump's security picks and early 2025 border policy directives for catalysts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus shows low implied probabilities for another US military strike on Venezuela, anchored by the absence of any announced US intentions amid post-election tensions. The disputed July presidential vote, upheld by Venezuela's Supreme Court in October, prompted fresh US sanctions on Maduro officials and PDVSA executives, reinforcing diplomatic pressure without kinetic escalation. US policy prioritizes recognizing opposition leader Edmundo González and supporting Guyana in the Essequibo border dispute via arbitration, not force. Upcoming US election outcomes and lame-duck foreign policy moves could shift dynamics, as could Venezuelan protests or regional mediation efforts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a US military strike on Cuba at near-zero implied probability, reflecting the absence of any official indications, troop movements, or escalatory rhetoric from US defense authorities amid longstanding tensions. Recent developments, including Russian naval visits to Havana in mid-2024 and Cuba's economic protests, have prompted US surveillance flights and diplomatic rebukes but no shift toward kinetic action—consistent with post-Cold War restraint since the 1962 missile crisis. Primary sources like Pentagon statements emphasize sanctions enforcement over force. Upcoming catalysts include potential post-US election policy reviews, though base rates favor diplomatic continuity over intervention.

question: Will Edmundo González Urrutia visit Venezuela by March 31, 2026?
description:
If the listed individual visits Venezuela between market creation and March 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as the individual physically entering the terrestrial territory of Venezuela. Whether or not the individual enters Venezuelan airspace or maritime territory during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for potential high-profile visits to Venezuela by March 31 hinges on US-Venezuela diplomatic maneuvering amid Nicolás Maduro's disputed reelection and inauguration on January 10. No official announcements confirm visits from key figures like Donald Trump, Marco Rubio, or JD Vance, despite Trump's campaign rhetoric on Venezuelan migrants and oil sanctions. Recent US non-recognition of Maduro's victory and ongoing opposition protests from María Corina Machado's camp elevate risks for any travel, keeping probabilities low across options. Upcoming Trump administration actions post-January 20 inauguration and potential prisoner swaps could catalyze shifts, but uncertainty persists given historical diplomatic freezes. Markets reflect trader caution on near-term breakthroughs.

If the listed individual visits Venezuela between market creation and March 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as the individual physically entering the terrestrial territory of Venezuela. Whether or not the individual enters Venezuelan airspace or maritime territory during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Will the U.S. invade Venezuela by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Venezuela between November 3, 2025 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Venezuela or the United States as of September 6, 2025, 12:00 PM ET, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

events.title: Will the U.S. invade Venezuela by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Venezuela between September 6 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Venezuela or the United States as of September 6, 2025, 12:00 PM ET, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a U.S. military invasion of Venezuela at very low odds, driven by the absence of any official U.S. preparations or statements signaling force amid competing global priorities like Ukraine and the Middle East. Key context includes post-July 2024 Venezuelan election fallout, where Washington recognized opposition gains, imposed fresh sanctions on Maduro officials and oil entities, and backed democratic transitions without military rhetoric. Diplomatic efforts via regional allies and economic pressure dominate U.S. Venezuela policy under Biden. The November U.S. presidential election looms as a potential catalyst—a Trump win might intensify tough talk on deportations and regime change, per past positions—but no troop buildups or defense shifts indicate near-term invasion risk, aligning with rare U.S. Latin America interventions.

question: Will the U.S. invade Cuba in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Cuban land territory by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Cuba or the United States as market creation, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

events.title: Will the U.S. invade Cuba in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Cuban land territory by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Cuba or the United States as market creation, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus leans heavily against a U.S. invasion of Cuba in 2026 at 75.5% odds on "No," driven by the absence of any official military plans, escalatory rhetoric, or strategic justifications from the Biden administration, congressional leaders, or 2024 presidential candidates. U.S.-Cuba relations remain tense but confined to economic sanctions, diplomatic pressure, and human rights condemnations, as seen in recent State Department responses to Cuba's widespread blackouts and protest suppressions in March 2024. Historical precedent—no direct military action since the 1961 Bay of Pigs—reinforces low risk, with focus instead on migration pacts and terrorism designations. Post-election policy shifts under a potential Trump return emphasize tighter sanctions over force, underscoring minimal geopolitical impetus for invasion amid global priorities like Ukraine and China.

question: Will the U.S. invade a Latin American country in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of the land territory of Latin American country by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by the relevant country or the United States as market creation, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

Qualifying Latin America countries: Belize, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Ecuador, Guyana, Paraguay, Peru, Suriname, Uruguay, Venezuela,, Cuba, Dominican Republic, Haiti

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Lack of any official U.S. military planning, deployments, or escalatory rhetoric toward Latin American nations underpins the 75.5% trader consensus against a 2026 invasion. No primary sources indicate shifts toward kinetic action amid focus on Ukraine, Middle East, and China rivalries. Historical restraint since the 1989 Panama operation, coupled with multilateral Haiti efforts, bolsters low-probability assessment, though regional elections or cartel violence could spark volatility.

question: Will USD-denominated stablecoin market share fall below 99% in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the USD-denominated stablecoin supply falls below 99% of total stablecoin supply at any point in 2026. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source is Artemis (https://app.artemisanalytics.com/stablecoins), using the “Stablecoin Supply by Currency” chart. The USD percentage shown when hovering over a monthly data point will be used.

A monthly data point will be considered finalized once the following month’s data point is published.

If the resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, the market will resolve based on other reliable crypto data sources.

question: Over $200M Hyperliquid buybacks in Q1?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the total USD value of Hyperliquid buybacks in Q1 is greater than $200M. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source will be the “Daily Buybacks” table on https://hyperscreener.asxn.xyz/revenue. The sum of the “Notional Amount” (USD) values from January 1 through March 31 will be used.

Daily data will be considered finalized once the following day’s data point is published.

If the resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, the market will resolve based on other reliable crypto data sources.

If the resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, the market will resolve based on other reliable crypto data sources.

question: Fact Check: Maduro capture staged?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if it is widely and credibly confirmed that the operation to capture Nicolás Maduro was staged or pre-arranged with Maduro (i.e., facilitated, permitted, or agreed to in advance such that Maduro’s capture was not the result of an adversarial raid) by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Statements from the U.S. government claiming that the operation was staged or pre-arranged will qualify for a "Yes" resolution. If no broad consensus has been reached by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Realistic shifts could arise from forensic video analysis, official investigations, or Maduro's next address confirming his status, though evidence tilts heavily against authenticity.

question: Will Tim Walz win the 2026 Minnesota Governor Democratic primary election?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Minnesota, scheduled to take place on August 11, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Minnesota Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Minnesota Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amy Klobuchar's commanding 93% implied probability in the Minnesota Democratic gubernatorial primary market stems from her enduring statewide popularity, high name recognition, and perceived openness to higher office amid incumbent Tim Walz's national vice presidential campaign diverting his focus. Recent polling aggregates show Klobuchar topping hypothetical Democratic fields, bolstering trader consensus on the 2026 primary still over a year away with no formal candidate announcements. Walz's low 1.7% odds reflect doubts over his return if the Harris ticket falters. Realistic challenges include Walz re-entering post-election, a strong alternative like Secretary of State Steve Simon surging, or Klobuchar confirming Senate reelection plans, any shifting the wisdom-of-crowds pricing.

question: Will Russia capture all of Prymorske by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the entirety of Prymorske, Zaporizhzhia Oblast, by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Prymorske will be considered captured if the entirety of the municipality is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

The territory of the municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities such as "Stepnohirsk" with a darker grey line. If all area within the municipality is shaded in red, however, the shading does not precisely match up with the border such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this will still qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Once Russia captures the entirety of Prymorske, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/K7mS2YU7bZoVVHga9

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map which will not be considered.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of if it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have made incremental advances toward Prymorske, a village in Donetsk Oblast's Velyka Novosilka sector, capturing nearby positions like parts of Vodyane amid ongoing artillery duels and drone strikes, per recent Institute for the Study of War assessments. Ukrainian defenses, bolstered by Western-supplied systems, have slowed the push despite manpower strains from mobilization delays. Trader consensus reflects caution, pricing low odds for full Russian capture by the deadline due to seasonal mud hindering mechanized assaults and Kyiv's fortified lines. question: San Diego State Aztecs vs. Utah State Aggies
description:
In the upcoming CBB game, scheduled for January 11 at 12:00 AM ET:

If the San Diego State Aztecs win, the market will resolve to "San Diego State Aztecs".

If the Utah State Aggies win, the market will resolve to "Utah State Aggies".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

events.title: San Diego State Aztecs vs. Utah State Aggies
events.description:

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' even 50% implied probability for San Diego State Aztecs over Utah State Aggies stems from their razor-thin Mountain West Conference records and mirrored defensive efficiencies, both ranking top-three in points allowed per possession. Recent form shows Utah State's home dominance—winning six straight in Logan—offset by San Diego State's road grit and superior rebounding margins in prior meetings. Competitive balance persists amid quiet injury reports, with no major absences confirmed via official updates. Odds could tip if Aztecs guard Darrion Trammell practices fully after a minor tweak or Aggies forward Great Osabor regains peak conditioning, amplifying their respective frontcourt edges in this pivotal late-season clash.

question: Delcy Rodríguez out as leader of Venezuela by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Acting President Delcy Rodríguez ceases to be leader of Venezuela for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Delcy Rodríguez's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Delcy Rodríguez and the government of Venezuela; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Delcy Rodríguez remains Venezuela's Executive Vice President and a dominant PSUV figure, anchoring trader consensus against her near-term ouster amid Nicolás Maduro's regime consolidation following the disputed July 28 election. Official statements confirm no leadership changes, despite international sanctions and opposition fraud claims led by María Corina Machado's camp, which have spurred protests but faced crackdowns. Recent cabinet tweaks excluded Rodríguez, signaling her stability, while U.S. pressure via oil sector actions has yielded limited internal shifts. Upcoming PSUV congress or Maduro's January 10 inauguration could test loyalties, but current low market odds reflect the regime's resilience and absence of verified dissent from Diosdado Cabello or other allies.

question: Will FalleN retire by June 1?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if FalleN, the professional Counter-Strike player, announces his retirement from professional competitive esports by June 1, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

An announcement from FalleN that he is retiring will qualify even if his official retirement has not yet occurred. 

Breaks, periods of inactivity, transitions to another game, or transitions to streaming will not count unless FalleN explicitly announces his retirement from professional competitive esports.

The resolution source will be official announcements from FalleN; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: US recognizes Reza Pahlavi as leader of Iran in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Trump administration announces that the United States officially recognizes Reza Pahlavi as the "leader of Iran" by December 31, 2026, 11:59 ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Roles that could qualify for "leader of Iran" status include, but are not limited to, "head of state," "prime minister," or other similar roles that give him primary executive authority in the territory of Iran.

A qualifying US statement must be direct and unqualified. Conditional, hypothetical, supportive, or implied statements do not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US government and Donald Trump, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

question: Will the U.S. invade Greenland in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of the land territory of Greenland by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, the land territory of Greenland refers to all territory recognized by Denmark as part of the autonomous territory of Greenland within the Kingdom of Denmark, as of market creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Will a coin launched in 2026 end the year above $20B FDV?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any token launched in 2026 ends the year with an FDV above $20B. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

A token must be actively and publicly transferable and tradable to be considered launched.

The FDV will be calculated by multiplying the total token supply by the token price. The token price used will be the “Close” price on CoinGecko for December 31, 2026, as shown in the token’s historical data (e.g., Hyperliquid: https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/hyperliquid/historical_data).

Stablecoins, liquid staking tokens (LSTs), liquidity pool tokens, and synthetic representations of other assets will not qualify.
